title: Vulnerable employment is higher among women than men in lower-income countries and economies
textParagraph: Vulnerable employment is common in low-income countries and economies. Among the poorest countries, vulnerable employment rates are as high as 90 percent, and with women more affected than men. In higher-income countries, vulnerable employment is less common, and the share of men in vulnerable employment tends to be higher than among women.
y_axis_title: Vulnerable employment (% of labor force) by gender
source: ILO Modelled Estimates database (ILOEST), International Labour Organization (ILO). Retrieved from Gender Data Portal (SL.EMP.VULN.FE.ZS; SL.EMP.VULN.MA.ZS)
visdescription: This is a range chart with two dots for men and women for every country. The x-axis shows the country’s income and is classified by its income group and the y-axis shows the rate of vulnerable employment for women and men.
keyFactLabel: Low and middle-income countries, vulnerable employment was higher among women than men
